pr.title: matched hsd_803B5C2C function in the hsd_3B34.c file
pr.author: asylum14

summary: Matched the previously undecompiled hsd_803B5C2C function in src/sysdolphin/baselib/hsd_3B34.c. The implementation adds a file-local static integer lbl_804D6398 initialized to 3 and a setter-like function that accepts a mode value, stores it, then clamps invalid values outside the inclusive range 1..10 back to the default value 3.
what_changed: Added static int lbl_804D6398 = 3; as file-local state associated with hsd_803B5C2C.
Implemented void hsd_803B5C2C(int mode), storing the incoming mode and resetting lbl_804D6398 to 3 if mode is less than 1 or greater than 10.
PR body states the function was matched 100% against assembly and describes the behavior as boundary checking 1 through 10 with default fallback 3.
smart_moves: Kept the unknown data symbol as lbl_804D6398 rather than inventing an unsupported semantic name, preserving decomp accuracy until stronger evidence exists.
Used a simple direct conditional, (mode < 1) || (mode > 10), matching the described assembly behavior without over-abstracting into a helper or macro.
Initialized the backing static variable to the same default value used by the invalid-input fallback, making the default state explicit in C.
decomp_lessons: For small baselib functions, a direct translation of global/static state writes plus boundary checks can be enough to achieve a complete match.
When semantic purpose is unclear, retaining autogenerated lbl_ names is preferable to premature naming; behavior can still be documented through surrounding code and PR notes.
Default fallback values should be represented both in static initialization and runtime validation when the assembly shows both concepts.
A setter that writes first and then validates may be important for matching instruction order; avoid simplifying to only assign once unless the generated assembly remains equivalent.
naming_conventions: Unidentified static data retained the label-style name lbl_804D6398.
The function kept its address-based placeholder name hsd_803B5C2C, consistent with unmatched or not-yet-semantically-named HSD/baselib symbols.
The file name hsd_3B34.c and included header hsd_3B34.h follow the address/block-oriented naming used for partially decompiled HSD baselib units.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Represented the range check explicitly as two comparisons joined by logical OR: mode < 1 or mode > 10.
Preserved the apparent assignment order by assigning lbl_804D6398 = mode before the invalid-range reset to 3.
Used a file-local static int initialized to 3 to model the data symbol lbl_804D6398.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that rename hsd_803B5C2C or lbl_804D6398 to semantic names.
Search for references to lbl_804D6398 to determine what subsystem or mode this global controls.
Check whether hsd_3B34.h declares hsd_803B5C2C and whether future PRs add related functions in hsd_3B34.c.
Look for similar HSD baselib setter functions that validate mode values against inclusive numeric ranges.
